reasoning: The reference value for the threshold is 1.25, meaning that a 30-day period must produce 13 or more 'Strategic developments' in Ireland to resolve YES (10 * 1.25 = 12.5). The baseline average over the past year is just 3 events per year (0.25 per 30 days). For a single 30-day period to see 13+ strategic developments, there would need to be an unprecedented event like a massive country-wide security sweep with dozens of separate arrests or significant non-violent tactical shifts. Recent news out of Ireland (July and August 2025) shows no such phenomenon. While there have been riots in neighboring Northern Ireland, ACLED codes Northern Ireland as part of the United Kingdom, so this will not affect the count for Ireland. Given the extreme multiplier required (over 50x the normal rate) and the lack of any current catalysts, the probability is extremely low and scales up only marginally over time.
